name: py-graphql-client

metadata.name: py-graphql-client
metadata.summary: A dead-simple GraphQL client that supports subscriptions over websockets

documentation:
# py-graphql-client
Dead-simple to use GraphQL client over websocket. Using the
[apollo-transport-ws](https://github.com/apollographql/subscriptions-transport-ws/blob/master/PROTOCOL.md)
protocol.

## Install

```bash
pip install py-graphql-client
```

## Examples

### Setup subscriptions super easily

```python
from graphql_client import GraphQLClient

query = """
  subscription {
    notifications {
      id
      title
      content
    }
  }
"""

with GraphQLClient('ws://localhost:8080/graphql') as client:

  sub_id = client.subscribe(query, callback=callback)
  # do other stuff
  # ...
  # later stop the subscription
  client.stop_subscribe(sub_id)

def callback(_id, data):
  print("got new data..")
  print(f"msg id: {_id}. data: {data}")
```

### Variables can be passed

```python
from graphql_client import GraphQLClient

query = """
    subscription ($limit: Int!) {
      notifications (order_by: {created: "desc"}, limit: $limit) {
        id
        title
        content
      }
    }
  """

with GraphQLClient('ws://localhost:8080/graphql') as client:
  sub_id = client.subscribe(query, variables={'limit': 10}, callback=callback)
  # ...

def callback(_id, data):
  print("got new data..")
  print(f"msg id: {_id}. data: {data}")

```

### Headers can be passed too

```python
from graphql_client import GraphQLClient

query = """
    subscription ($limit: Int!) {
      notifications (order_by: {created: "desc"}, limit: $limit) {
        id
        title
        content
      }
    }
  """

with GraphQLClient('ws://localhost:8080/graphql') as client:
  sub_id = client.subscribe(query,
                            variables={'limit': 10},
                            headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer xxxx'},
                            callback=callback)
  ...
  client.stop_subscribe(sub_id)

def callback(_id, data):
  print("got new data..")
  print(f"msg id: {_id}. data: {data}")
```

### Normal queries and mutations work too

```python
from graphql_client import GraphQLClient

query = """
  query ($limit: Int!) {
    notifications (order_by: {created: "desc"}, limit: $limit) {
      id
      title
      content
    }
  }
"""

with GraphQLClient('ws://localhost:8080/graphql') as client:
    res = client.query(query, variables={'limit': 10}, headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer xxxx'})
    print(res)
```

### Without the context manager API

```python
from graphql_client import GraphQLClient

query = """
  query ($limit: Int!) {
    notifications (order_by: {created: "desc"}, limit: $limit) {
      id
      title
      content
    }
  }
"""

client = GraphQLClient('ws://localhost:8080/graphql')
res = client.query(query, variables={'limit': 10}, headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer xxxx'})
print(res)
client.close()
```


## TODO
- support http as well
- should use asyncio websocket library?

modules.name: graphql_client
documentation:
A simple GraphQL client that works over Websocket as the transport
protocol, instead of HTTP.
This follows the Apollo protocol.
https://github.com/apollographql/subscriptions-transport-ws/blob/master/PROTOCOL.md

classes.name: graphql_client.ConnectionException
documentation: Exception thrown during connection errors to the GraphQL server

classes.name: graphql_client.InvalidPayloadException
documentation: Exception thrown if payload recived from server is mal-formed or cannot be parsed

classes.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient

methods.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient.connect
documentation: Initializes a connection with the server.

methods.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient._receiver_task
documentation:
the recieve function of the client. Which validates response from the
server and queues data
wait for any valid message, while ignoring GQL_CONNECTION_KEEP_ALIVE

methods.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient._start
documentation: pass a callback function only if this is a subscription

methods.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient.query
documentation:
Run a GraphQL query or mutation. The `query` argument is a GraphQL query
string. You can pass optional variables and headers.

PS: To run a subscription, see the `subscribe` method.

methods.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient.subscribe
documentation:
Run a GraphQL subscription.

Parameters:
query (str): the GraphQL query string
callback (function): a callback function. This is mandatory.
This callback function is called, everytime there is new data from the
subscription.
variables (dict): (optional) GraphQL variables
headers (dict): (optional) a dictionary of headers for the session

Returns:
op_id (str): The operation id (a UUIDv4) for this subscription operation

methods.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient.stop_subscribe
documentation:
Stop a subscription. Takes an operation ID (`op_id`) and stops the
subscription.

methods.name: graphql_client.GraphQLClient.close
documentation:
Close the connection with the server. To reconnect, use the `connect`
method.

A simple GraphQL client that works over Websocket as the transport
protocol, instead of HTTP.
This follows the Apollo protocol.
https://github.com/apollographql/subscriptions-transport-ws/blob/master/PROTOCOL.md
